title: Request Targets, Host Authority, Proxy Trust, and Client Identity · Independent Type Trial
summary: An independent service team receives a different tenant, traffic, trust, persistence, and failure profile and transfers request targets, host authority, proxy trust, and client identity into a new request authority and trusted-proxy decision record. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of request authority and trusted-proxy decision record with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugg…
objectives: Interpret method, url, originalUrl, path, protocol, secure, hostname, host, ip, ips, headers, route, params, query, body, and socket using server-side semantics.
Validate canonical host and port against configured authority before routing, link generation, cache keys, redirects, or tenant selection.
Configure trust proxy by explicit addresses or hop logic and derive client, protocol, and host only when every trusted hop overwrites forwarded fields.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Node and Express server Commit to the mission model · Lab Express Request Field Meaning
steps.instruction:
lab case — A lab team handles case 8727 in a multi-tenant case service with a bounded PostgreSQL pool. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to derive authority only across an explicit trusted-proxy and host allow-list boundary; keep peak memory below the stated byte ceiling; then emit a server error and retain one low-cardinality causal trace. Browser code models pure decisions only. Node, Express, listener, TLS, PostgreSQL, proxy, worker, load, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Node and Express server Commit to the mission model · Lab Express Request Field Meaning.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for interpret method, url, originalurl, path, protocol, secure, hostname, host, ip, ips, headers, route, params, query, body, and socket using server-side semantics. For Node and Express server Commit to the mission model · Lab Express Request Field Meaning, keep Express Request Field Meaning connected to executable HTTP client decision evidence.
